notes: Version compatibility contract between openclaw-onboarding and blackceo-command-center. fleet-refresh.sh reads pinnedTag (or resolves latest >= minVersion when pinnedTag is null) and checks out that tag. The skill-32 installer refuses to clone a CC older than minVersion. CI fails if onboardingVersion != /version or if pinnedTag < minVersion. v11.19.0 adds self-service add+wire (roles/SOPs/personas + converge); requires CC v4.39.0 for /api/system/converge endpoint. Skill 48 (facebook-ad-generator) lands ad runs on the Kanban board via the producer-side caller scripts/cc_board.py, which requires the POST/PATCH /api/ad-campaigns endpoints introduced in CC v4.50.0 (merge 89b6d5e); minVersion + pinnedTag are bumped to v4.50.0 so fleet-refresh + the skill-32 installer guarantee the endpoint is present. The board hookup is FAIL-SOFT: on a CC older than minVersion (or an unreachable board) the run degrades to ungrouped cards and logs it (INSTALL.md graceful degradation), it never fails an ad job. If CC is older, converge falls back to on-box seed-workspaces.py + ingest-sop-library.sh (graceful degradation, §1.6 step 5). v16.2.4 bumps minVersion + pinnedTag to v4.56.1 (current CC release as of 2026-06-29; fixes fleet-refresh deploying stale CC v4.50.0 to every box). v16.4.0 registers Skill 50 (email-engine): the Email Engine authors DRAFT-ONLY email jobs and hands them to the Skill-44 Convert & Flow deploy path; it ships NO board caller of its own (an email job is board-visible only when carded onto the existing marketing Kanban lane via mc-route) — it introduces NO new CC endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change, so minVersion + pinnedTag are UNCHANGED (v4.56.1). The board hookup is FAIL-SOFT: on an unreachable board the run degrades to a local draft plan and logs it, it never fails an email job. Nothing is sent without explicit human approval. v16.5.0 registers Skill 49 (signature-funnel): the Signature Funnel AUTHORS the SACRED 12-section Hero copy + the per-section image prompts inside its own fail-closed pipeline, then DELEGATES image generation to Skill 47 and ALL GoHighLevel media + funnel/page build to Skill 6 (the ONE GHL delivery rail) via Skill 6's EXISTING REST canvas / builder + media endpoints; a 'signature funnel' request routes to it through the new shared STEP-0 funnel-engine selector registry in Skill 6 (06-ghl-install-pages/funnel-engines/registry.json). It introduces NO new Command Center endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change (Skill 49 ships NO board caller; a funnel job is board-visible only when carded onto the existing web-development / QC Kanban lanes via mc-route, fail-soft), so minVersion + pinnedTag are UNCHANGED (v4.56.1). Publish is human-approved (preview URLs + labeled ~/Downloads bundle only); nothing is published without explicit human approval. v16.6.0 registers Skill 55 (product-bio): the Product Bio Engine turns a 4-field intake into the 6,000-7,000-word, 10-section master-brain product bio + its Google-Docs-importable HTML as a LOCAL-ONLY labeled ~/Downloads deliverable on the CLIENT's own model providers (never Anthropic, never operator keys), replacing the retired 25-node n8n / Google Drive / Slack / Gmail workflow. It introduces NO new Command Center endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change (Skill 55 ships NO board caller; a product-bio job is board-visible only when carded onto the existing tasks / marketing Kanban lane via mc-route, fail-soft), so minVersion + pinnedTag are UNCHANGED (v4.56.1). Delivery is local + human-reviewed; nothing is sent to any external service (no n8n / Drive / Slack / Gmail / Airtable) at runtime. v16.7.0 registers Skill 52 (avatar-alchemist): the Avatar Alchemist brand-intelligence engine turns ONE completed brand-intake interview into the full brand-intelligence package (40 generators across 7 subsystems -> 16 named deliverables / 37 documents) as a LOCAL-ONLY labeled ~/Downloads bundle with a signed provenance certificate, on the CLIENT's own model providers (never Anthropic, never operator keys), replacing the retired 233-node n8n / Airtable / Google Drive / Slack / Gmail workflow. A Book/Brand version selector runs FIRST (version=book routes to Skill 53 or parks fail-closed, never the brand pipeline). Downstream handoffs (bot docs -> Skill 38; Top-39 / headline / targeting -> Skill 48; images -> Skill 47; GHL delivery -> Skill 6) all ride EXISTING rails; Skill 52 ships NO board caller; a brand-intelligence job is board-visible only when carded onto the existing tasks / marketing Kanban lane via mc-route (fail-soft). It introduces NO new Command Center endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change, so minVersion + pinnedTag are UNCHANGED (v4.56.1). Cross-linked with (never merged into) Skill 55; the tone subsystem is a lockstep copy of the shared shared-utils/tone-writing-core/ referenced by Skills 53/54. v16.8.0 registers Skill 56 (sales-page-assets): the Direct-Response sibling of Skill 49 AUTHORS the 8-section main A/B + countdown, the 9-section Trevor Otts upsell, the downsell, the Sovereign Architect high-ticket long-form, the order-bump copy, and the slice-covered image plan inside its own fail-closed pipeline, then DELEGATES image generation to Skill 47 (or the client's own image provider) and ALL GoHighLevel media + funnel/page build to Skill 6 (the ONE GHL delivery rail) via Skill 6's EXISTING REST canvas / builder + media endpoints, and routes the bump to Skill 44; a 'sales page assets' / direct-response A/B request routes to it through the shared STEP-0 funnel-engine selector registry in Skill 6 (06-ghl-install-pages/funnel-engines/registry.json) as the SECOND registered engine. It introduces NO new Command Center endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change, so minVersion + pinnedTag are UNCHANGED (v4.56.1). Client runtime uses the CLIENT's own model providers (never Anthropic, never operator keys); publish is human-approved. v16.10.0 registers Skill 53 (book-writer) and Skill 54 (anthology-writer) as a combined train: Skill 53 is the BOOK version of the Avatar Alchemist (Skill 52 is the BRAND version; the shared Book/Brand selector routes version=book to Skill 53, version=brand to Skill 52) and turns ONE completed book-intake interview into a tone-matched 12-chapter nonfiction book (or a 4x3x3 offer book handed to Skill 51) plus companion assets as a LOCAL-ONLY labeled ~/Downloads bundle with a signed PROCESS-CERTIFICATE; Skill 54 is the SEPARATE sibling multi-contributor anthology engine (never consolidated with Skill 53) that turns ONE contributor intake into one gated chapter in that contributor's blended voice, also delivered as a labeled LOCAL bundle. Both bake a lockstep copy of the shared shared-utils/tone-writing-core/ (proved by verify_tone_core_sync.py) and gate every SACRED count/floor with fail-closed model-free Python provers that MEASURE the stripped text (self-reported counts ignored). Both replace their respective source n8n / Airtable / Google Drive-Docs / Slack / Gmail workflows with LOCAL-ONLY pipelines on the CLIENT's own model providers — never Anthropic, never operator keys. A book-writer or anthology-writer job rides the existing tasks / marketing Kanban lane (fail-soft); it introduces NO new Command Center endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change, so minVersion + pinnedTag are UNCHANGED (v4.56.1). Standalone — neither ships a role-library or universal-sops footprint; no prerequisite skill for either. v16.3.0 registered Skill 51 (signature-presentation). FIX-PRES-06 (v17.0.27) rewrites this note to the CURRENT scope: Skill 51 is now the presentations METHODOLOGY layer whose governed deck pipeline (build_deck.py + run_signature_deck.py, materialized under Skill 23) DOES card the deck run onto the Command Center via the producer-side caller scripts/cc_board.py (run-begin ingest_deck_task, per-phase patch_phase advances, terminal close), and DEPENDS on the CC presentations DONE-GATE: a task-PATCH carrying {phase_id, status, process_certificate_sha} that promotes a card to done ONLY when a valid PROCESS-CERTIFICATE sha (minted by prove-deck.py) is attached. That done-gate was introduced in blackceo-command-center v4.56.0 (recorded in this repo README.md and CHANGELOG.md). Confirmation: the done-gate and the {phase_id, status, process_certificate_sha} task-PATCH shape were introduced in CC v4.56.0 and exist exactly as the producer assumes at every pinned tag at or above it (both v4.59.1 and the current pin v4.69.1 include it), so the pin already guarantees it. Skill 51 still introduces NO NEW Command Center endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change beyond consuming that pre-existing done-gate, so minVersion is UNCHANGED (v4.59.1) while pinnedTag is bumped to v4.69.1 (see the v19.0.0 note at the end of these notes). The board hookup is FAIL-SOFT (a disabled/unreachable board degrades to ungrouped and logs it; it never fails a deck build); delivery is local + human-reviewed; publish is human-approved. v16.9.0 registers Skill 57 (social-media-in-a-box): the Social Media-in-a-Box engine AUTHORS the 30-day content calendar, platform-specific post copy, visual content specs, and posting schedule inside its own fail-closed pipeline, then DELEGATES media generation to Skill 47 and platform integration to Skill 6 via EXISTING REST endpoints. It introduces NO new Command Center endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change (Skill 57 ships NO board caller; content calendar and posts are board-visible only when carded onto existing marketing Kanban lanes via mc-route, fail-soft), so minVersion + pinnedTag are UNCHANGED (v4.56.1). Client runtime uses the CLIENT's own model providers (never Anthropic, never operator keys); publish is human-approved. v19.0.0 bumps pinnedTag from v4.59.1 to v4.69.1 (the latest CC release, == the operator/canary Command Center) WITHOUT changing minVersion (kept permissive at v4.59.1 so the v19 fleet roll's assert_min_version does not block boxes still on an older CC mid-update). This is a CORRECTNESS FIX: resolve_cc_tag returns pinnedTag unconditionally, so the stale v4.59.1 pin made fleet-refresh `git checkout v4.59.1` and DOWNGRADE any box already above it; pinning v4.69.1 makes the roll deploy the canary-matched CC instead. v4.69.1 >= minVersion (v4.59.1) and maxVersion is null, so the schema contract (pinnedTag >= minVersion, pinnedTag <= maxVersion-or-unbounded) holds. v20.0.2 bumps pinnedTag from v4.69.1 to v6.0.0 (the current Command Center major release) while keeping minVersion UNCHANGED (permissive at v4.59.1 so the fleet roll's assert_min_version does not block boxes still on an older CC mid-update). Same CORRECTNESS rationale as the v19.0.0 note above: resolve_cc_tag returns pinnedTag unconditionally, so the stale v4.69.1 pin would make fleet-refresh `git checkout v4.69.1` and DOWNGRADE any box already on v6.0.0; pinning v6.0.0 makes the roll deploy the current CC instead. v6.0.0 >= minVersion (v4.59.1) and maxVersion is null, so the schema contract (pinnedTag >= minVersion, pinnedTag <= maxVersion-or-unbounded) holds. v20.0.7 bumps pinnedTag from v6.0.0 to v6.0.1: v6.0.1 is a one-line portability fix ONLY (scripts/atomic-deploy.sh's mktemp call at line 428 lacked the XXXXXX template GNU coreutils mktemp requires, so Phase 2 of the atomic deploy failed closed on every Linux/VPS fleet box; macOS BSD mktemp tolerated the missing template, which is why it never surfaced on the operator Mac) — no app code, no endpoint, and no mission-control.db schema changed, so minVersion stays UNCHANGED (v4.59.1, permissive) and the schema contract (pinnedTag >= minVersion, pinnedTag <= maxVersion-or-unbounded) still holds. Annotated tag v6.0.1 (blackceo-command-center) is cut at commit d4abf9436ff47a2c1aaadb8b11bd335e9c5551b8, verified ahead-by-2/behind-by-0 of v6.0.0 with the delta scoped to CHANGELOG.md + package.json + package-lock.json + version (metadata) and the single mktemp line — same CORRECTNESS rationale as the v19.0.0/v20.0.2 notes above: resolve_cc_tag returns pinnedTag unconditionally, so leaving the stale v6.0.0 pin in place would keep shipping the un-fixed atomic-deploy.sh to every VPS fleet box on the next roll. On branch skill62/cinematic-engine (NOT yet merged to main; no version/tag ripple until the merge ticket lands, see the engine's own U25/U27 units): registers Skill 62 (cinematic-web-funnel-engine) as the client-facing web-development capability that builds conversion-focused cinematic websites, landing pages, and funnels (AI-generated scroll-controlled scene sequences on the client's own Kie.ai key, real encoded boundary frames, a production Next.js/TypeScript site, Vercel deployment, GoHighLevel/Convert and Flow forms/calendars/payments/workflows). A cinematic/animated/immersive/scroll request routes to it through the SAME shared STEP-0 funnel-engine selector registry Skills 49/56 already use (06-ghl-install-pages/funnel-engines/registry.json) as the THIRD registered engine; protected copy methodologies (Signature Funnel / Sales-Page-Assets) are DELEGATED to Skills 49/56 rather than duplicated when the client names one explicitly. It introduces NO new Command Center endpoint and NO mission-control.db schema change (ships NO board caller of its own; a build is board-visible only when carded onto the existing web-development Kanban lane via mc-route, fail-soft, per the existing done-gate task-PATCH {phase_id, status, process_certificate_sha} already present at the pinned tag), so minVersion + pinnedTag are UNCHANGED (v4.59.1 / v6.0.2). Command Center generic discovery (matchSkillsForTask()) no-change integration is proved separately (deferred to this engine's own U23 unit) before any Command Center change is even considered. Client runtime uses the CLIENT's own configured providers (Kie.ai on the client's own KIE_API_KEY; never Anthropic, never operator keys); no paid generation without an approved budget cap. Publish (Vercel preview -> production, and any GHL iframe embed) is human-approved.
